Title: Goldilocks zone of lignin: Two extremes of valve lignification lead to silique indehiscence in Brassicaceae
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive); native ABI3 promoter for reporter
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: silique valve endocarp b layer; lignified layer; valve margin/mesocarp in canola overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: provides rigidity/resistance in silique valves enabling or modulating tensile-force-driven dehiscence; increased lignification confers shatter tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: lignin biosynthesis / secondary cell wall formation in silique valve tissues
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates NST1 and NST3 expression; promotes expression of lignin biosynthetic-process genes indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA; ABA signaling via SnRK2.2/2.3/2.6 inferred upstream
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: valve-tissue-restricted master regulator upstream of NST1/3 determining degree of silique lignification and thus shatter tolerance/indehiscence
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss of ABI3 reduces endocarp b lignification; overexpression increases valve lignification and causes indehiscent/shatter-tolerant siliques in Arabidopsis and canola
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis (abi3-6); 35S overexpression in Arabidopsis and canola; histology/TBO and phloroglucinol staining; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; promoter-YFP reporter; ChIP-PCR; shatter assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in Arabidopsis silique valve tissues during 6-9 DAP, including mesocarp, endocarp a, then endocarp b
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: strong in silique mesocarp and endocarp a at 6 DAP and endocarp b at 9 DAP in Arabidopsis; overexpression evaluated in canola valves
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: silique maturation/drying; high humidity used for ABA-pathway mutant maintenance; mature pod shatter testing after drying/acclimation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABI3 is required and sufficient for lignification of the silique endocarp b and lignified layer. 2. ABI3 directly binds RY motifs in NST1 and NST3 promoters and activates their expression. 3. ABI3 overexpression increases lignification and confers shatter tolerance/indehiscence in Arabidopsis and canola.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong candidate for engineering shatter-tolerant canola through reproductive-tissue lignification without disrupting dehiscence-zone patterning; preferable over direct NST1/3 overexpression due to fewer ectopic lignification risks
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ABI3-OX canola produced larger-mass, wider valves; potential reproductive-tissue effects linked to known seed maturation roles, but no valve patterning defect reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ABI3 acts independently of patterning genes for tissue identity, but genetically functions at or downstream of IND and SHP1/2 for enb lignification; overexpression did not rescue patterning defects of ind-1 or shp1/2 mutants

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: silique valve endocarp b layer and lignified layer
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: provides secondary wall strengthening required to tune pod shattering
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: secondary cell wall formation / lignin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes secondary wall biosynthetic/lignin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NST3; ABI3; SnRK2.2/2.3/2.6 (literature-supported upstream kinase, discussed)
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: required downstream effector of ABI3 for valve lignification; overexpression alone is sufficient to cause excess lignification and indehiscence
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: nst1/3 double knockout abolishes enb lignification and causes indehiscent siliques; NST1 overexpression increases lignification and shatter tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: double mutant analysis with nst3; 35S overexpression; histology; RNA-seq comparison with abi3-6; shatter scoring
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: known valve lignification regulator; expression reduced in abi3-6 siliques
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: downregulated in abi3-6 at 3 and 9 DAP; overexpressed transgenically
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: silique developmental stages 3-12 DAP and maturation/drying
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NST1 transcript abundance is reduced in abi3 mutants. 2. NST1/NST3 are required downstream of ABI3 for valve lignification. 3. NST1 overexpression causes strong enb lignification and shatter tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: can enhance silique lignification and shatter tolerance, but likely less suitable than ABI3 due to broader lignification roles and ectopic lignification risk
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: direct overexpression may cause severe developmental anomalies due to ectopic lignification, as discussed
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: studied mainly with NST3 as partially redundant pair; ABI3 overexpression cannot restore lignification in nst1/3 mutant background

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: silique valve endocarp b layer and lignified layer
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: provides secondary wall strengthening required to tune pod shattering
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: secondary cell wall formation / lignin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes secondary wall biosynthetic/lignin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: required downstream effector of ABI3 for valve lignification; strong determinant of no-lignin indehiscence when absent with NST1 and of excess-lignin indehiscence when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: nst1/3 double knockout abolishes enb lignification and causes indehiscent siliques; NST3 overexpression increases lignification and shatter tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: double mutant analysis with nst1; 35S overexpression; ChIP-PCR for ABI3 binding to NST3 promoter; histology; RNA-seq; shatter scoring
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expression reduced strongly in abi3-6 siliques
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: downregulated in abi3-6 at 3 and 9 DAP; overexpressed transgenically
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: silique developmental stages 3-12 DAP and maturation/drying
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NST3 is a direct transcriptional target of ABI3 via promoter RY motifs. 2. NST1/NST3 are required for silique valve lignification downstream of ABI3. 3. NST3 overexpression causes strong enb lignification and shatter tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for increasing shatter tolerance via lignification, but less preferred than ABI3 because direct NST3 overexpression may induce undesirable ectopic lignification
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: direct overexpression may cause severe developmental anomalies due to ectopic lignification, as discussed
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NST3 promoter contains three RY motifs enriched in ABI3 ChIP-PCR
